Hey everyone, this is Russell. I want to welcome you guys to the training webinar. I apologize for starting a few minutes late. I was just getting all the pieces and the notes and everything together so that we can cover everything. It's going to be awesome for you guys. So, glad you got a lot of you guys on. So, I appreciate you guys jumping on. I know it's kind of last minute. I just spent a couple of weeks since I've talked to you guys and I felt bad and I wanted to do something really cool before the weekend. I had a couple of you guys who were actually actively working on your webinar presentations right now. I thought, you know what? I think this would be a really fun thing to do. So, that is the game plan. Let me show it out of my Skype really quick so there's no noises that interrupt us. Alright, so I'm excited you guys. This is going to be a lot of fun. So, what are we going to talk about today is we're going to be talking about how to structure a sales webinar. I call this the perfect webinar. The reason why I call it perfect webinar is because I've literally been doing webinars and stage presentations for, man, I say, it's getting pretty dang close to 10 years. So, about a decade, but not quite that long. And I tell you that learning how to do this the right way was not necessarily an easy process. It was way different than I thought and I learned a lot along the way. So, I want to just shortcut you guys and just give you the template and the script for what I think is the perfect webinar. So, that way the first time out of the gate, or maybe not the first, but close to the first time out of the gate, you can have the perfect webinar that you can get on and you can sell people and make some money really, really quickly. I honestly think one of the fastest ways to make money on the internet is to do a webinar. If you said, hey Russell, I need you to make 100 grand tomorrow or else I'm going to kidnap your family, I would put together a webinar. That would be it. I'm going to show you guys, kind of go through everything that I've learned over the last 10 years to do these. And then I'm going to actually show you how I did this here with one of my webinars I did recently called High Ticket Secrets. A lot of you guys were on that webinar and so you'll kind of see this madness in action, how the whole thing kind of worked together. So, that is the game plan. Now, basically when I first learned about this, I was dabbling on the internet. I began probably about 12 years ago or so. I was learning some stuff and it took me about two years before I was making any money and I started growing and I was having a bunch of fun. And by the time I saw this guy doing this internet marketing seminar. Now, I'd never been to an internet marketing seminar before, but for me at the time, I was like, I was kind of like this lonely kid. They always say that being an entrepreneur is like the loneliest job in the world because nobody really understands what you do or who you are or why you're so passionate about these crazy ideas, right? I'm sure some of you guys probably feel that way, am I right? Being an entrepreneur is lonely and that's how I was. And I was like, man, there's going to be all these people who actually do what I do and care about this stuff. They're going to be together in one room. I want to go to it. It was $2,000. It was super expensive for me at the time. But I paid, I went out to this event and it was really cool. I had a chance to just meet some awesome people and it was one of the coolest things I did. In fact, if you guys have a chance to go to an event, go to an event. Those of you guys who came to our last boys' event, you know the power of being in an event. Just being around people who look at life differently, who look at life the way you do, can just do huge things for your business and for what you're trying to accomplish. And so I was at this event and I remember I thought I was going to go in for three days just have people speak to me and teach me cool stuff. That was kind of how I thought an event should be and what it was going to be, right? And so I go to this event and the first speaker gets up and he starts talking and he talks about 65, 70 minutes. And they start selling, starts talking about this product he has. I'm like, that's kind of weird. And then he keeps going and going and at the very end he sold this product and the product was $2,000. And I was like, wow, that's a lot of, I can't believe he's selling a $2,000 product. I spent $2,000 to get here. That was like the last of my money. This guy's selling everyone in here $2,000 product. And as soon as he closed, he finished his presentation. I looked and people started popping up from all around this room. Made about 500 people in the room and they're popping up and they all start running towards the back. I thought, this, this is fascinating. I can't believe what's happening. I looked in the back and I started counting. I was counting the people like 2004, six, eight, ten. I was doing the math and I was like, that guy just made $50,000 in one 90 minute presentation. I remember sitting there just like, wow, this is, I was just so fascinated watching the process. And the next person gets up, next speaker gets up, he talks about some stuff, shares his best ideas. And then boom, he closes his cell. His was like $5,000. And people are popping up all around the room, running to the back and they bought his thing. And the next speaker up, the next speaker, and every single time I'm doing the math, I'm averaging, trying to total up how much each of these speakers are making. And at the end of the event, I realized that at the event, okay, again, I'd spent $2,000 to be there. The event promoter had made over a million dollars selling things at this event. And the way that those seminars typically work is the person who puts on the event brings in speakers and then the speaker sells their product, the speaker gets half the money and the event promoter gets half the money. So there's over a million dollars made at this event and $500,000 went to the dude who was running the event and the other $500,000 went to the speakers. And I remember sitting there thinking, I got to learn how to be a speaker. Like this is crazy. And at the time, and some of you guys probably won't believe this, but I was a super, super shy kid. In fact, in my own personal, like my real life, I'm a very shy person, but I learned how to be a speaker and learn how to present the right way because I wanted to make money. Okay, I'm a capitalist just like all of us, right? I want to make money. I saw these guys are making crazy money doing hardly anything. I want to do it. And so I started trying to go out there and learn how to become a public speaker. And what was interesting is the very first event I went to after that, I was watching, I just wanted to see some other people do it so I could kind of get the gist of how it worked. And I vividly remember looking at these speakers and as they were teaching stuff, I'd say, man, I can teach better stuff than this. My stories are better. My content is better. Like these guys aren't even teaching that good of stuff. I remember being frustrated. And so finally, my very first chance I had to speak on stage, I got all excited and went up there and I had all my content laid out and like all my best stuff, all my best ideas that I ever had put in this presentation. And I wanted to wow the audience. I went up there and I shared with them so much stuff way more than anybody else had ever, had I ever seen give it an event. And I was like, these guys are going to love me so much. They are going to come and buy my stuff. And I just blew their minds with all this just awesomeness. And guess what happened? Nothing. Nobody moved. I closed at the end and I sold my product. Nobody went to the back of the room. And it was embarrassing. I literally standing at the stage, watching nobody pop up, nobody moved. And I then quietly walked to the back of the audience and people kind of shuffled out and left for lunch. And it was the most awkward, worst feeling I've ever felt in my life. I was so embarrassed. I literally walked out of that room, walked to my hotel room and I shut the door. And for three days, the rest of the days of it, I sat in the room. I was too embarrassed to come back down. I remember sitting there and I was so frustrated and so angry. And part of me just wanted to quit. You know, I said, it doesn't make sense. It's not fair. Like I paid my dues. I've done this. I've done this. I've, you know, I've, you know, I, the stuff I taught was amazing. Why in the world are these people buying it? I was so just frustrated and just heartened. And luckily for me, I decided not to quit. I decided, you know, I'm going to try it again. I got to figure this out. People are doing it. They can't be that much smarter than me. Okay. Sure, I barely graduated from school, but these guys don't seem that much smarter than me. I got to be able to figure it out. So then I got asked to speak at another event and the promoter asked me how I did the last event. And I told him I did all right. And I didn't really tell him any of the stats because he probably would have told me that I wasn't welcome to speak because that's when they make their money as well. Right. And so I went to the next event and I remember watching their speakers and the speakers would do their, their clothes and always drove me crazy. They're like, you know, I'm going to start the product at $10,000, but it's not 10,000. It's only 9,000. It's not 9,000. It's only eight. It's not eight. It's only seven. They kept going through and drop down, drop down to the point where the price gets down to zero, you know, to whatever $2,000. And I thought it was so cheesy. Like that's so used car sales. I'm like, what in the world? Like why would, why would, uh, why would anybody ever in a million years buy that, buy that thing? Right. That was kind of my, my thought, but people were on the back. And so when I got to get my presentation, um, I remember like the thing I said is I got to my clothes and I said, I'm not going to do that cheesy thing that most speakers do where they, where they increase the price and then, and then chop it down. I'm not going to do that. I'm not going to insult your guys intelligence. I said that I said, I'm not going to insult your intelligence. I'm just going to tell you what the price is. And I told people the price and guess what happened? Nothing again. And I was like, man, I'm so smart yet I'm the only dude in the room not making any money. You know what? Maybe I, maybe I should humble myself and start asking some questions and start finding out from these people that are successful. Why they're successful. Um, so after spending another three days in hotel room, uh, hiding from the audience, because I was so embarrassed. Um, I started looking out there to find out what people were doing, who was being successful. I got to find out how, how this whole game works. And so I started buying every public speaking course I could find, not like how to be a public speaker, because that wasn't my goal. My goal, like I can stand in front of an audience and speak well. Um, well, maybe some of you guys would argue that I speak too fast, that I slur my words. I say, I'm way too much. All those things are true. I'm not saying I want to become a great public speaker. I want to become somebody who could stand in front of a room, deliver a presentation and make money, close some sales. And, uh, and so I started buying, uh, you know, how to sell from stage programs from some of the best people in the world, guys like Armand Moore and, uh, Bill Glazer, Dan Kennedy. And I started buying all these courses and, and it was interesting as I would go through them. They, they were so counterintuitive to what I learned, what I, what I assumed. They always said like the person who teaches the most does not make the most money. Okay. Teaching is good. There's a time and a purpose for that. Okay. Those of you guys who came to Boise, you saw the time and the purpose. I spent three days teaching everything I had. I didn't sell anything. Okay. That was, that was an event where I was trying to, to, to fulfill and to, to give value. But when you're trying to sell something, it's different. And if you just give everything up front, um, people won't buy. Okay. They get overwhelmed if you give them too much stuff and they're so overwhelmed, they're not going to buy your product. Cause they felt like, well, I'm so overwhelmed. I got enough stuff I can go work on. They're not going to buy your thing. And so there's a, uh, there was a way to structure your content where people receive value, but, um, it was useful, but incomplete. It was structured and created in a way that the only logical thing to do at the end of the presentation was to buy, to buy the coaching program or buy the product. Wherever the thing was that, uh, it was being sold on the next step. And so I literally started studying from all these guys and, and every, every course I would go through, I would learn the next piece. I learned that a little thing here and a little thing there. And, and I kept adding this to my, to my presentations and, and I would learn a way to do, do my clothes and I would learn a way to structure my content, ways to introduce it. And, and, um, I learned from so many amazing people. Guys like, um, Jason Flatlin is another one who just taught me some amazing stuff. Um, um, Ted Thomas taught me some amazing stuff. Like so many pieces of that. And so I wish I could give credit to, in fact, maybe I might do that as I go through this presentation, give credit to, to different people as, as different pieces of this came about. But this is definitely not my invention. This is, this is me learning from, from probably 15 different mentors of some of the best stage presenters in the world, taking back all the pieces and kind of creating this into my, into what I do now. And whenever I create a webinar now, you guys, this is what I do. I print out this little perfect webinar sheet. I have it sitting down next to me and then I get out my slides and start building out the slides. And so you'll see this high ticket secrets product is a webinar that I'm going to go through. Now, just kind of not to sell you on why webinars are amazing, but to sell you on why webinars are amazing. You guys are fired up now. All right. Again, the guys, this tool will serve you for the rest of your life. In fact, one day I told some of the guys this story before, but we had four or five years ago are, we had our business. We built up to a hundred employees and one day literally out of the blue, all of our merchant accounts got shut down. I couldn't pay payroll. I couldn't pay staff for a year. I was trying to save this company. It was collapsing around us to the point where we had to lay off tons of people and we had to move from one building to the next. And the building we were in initially was about 20 or 25,000 square feet and we're moving into a 2,500 square feet office. And so we're moving all this stuff. We're throwing away just all of our, I mean, we literally threw away hundreds of computers and phones. And it was probably one of the saddest days of my life, to be honest. Like one of the most depressing, hardest to deal with. And I remember we were moving into this new little office and I just let off, you know, 80 employees and was trying to figure out what our next step was going to be. And we had literally had no money to even pay the rent on this new place we're moving into. And I thought, I got to figure out something. I've got to make some money. I got to make money now. And luckily I had a list of people and I sent an email out to this list. I said, Hey, I'm doing a webinar tomorrow. If you want to be on it, it's going to be awesome. The next day, which was the last day we had in our office, so we still had an internet connection. All the desks have been moved out. I had one desk left in the middle of the office. I had a little laptop on it. And I remember I was sitting there. I only had socks on. I must have taken my shoes off for some reason. And that morning I put together this presentation. I followed this format. I did the webinar. And at the end of the webinar, I closed and people started buying and they started buying and they started buying. And that one webinar, that one presentation made me $150,000. It gave us the cash we needed to move into our new building, pay the rent for a couple months, get everything restructured and give us the foundation we needed to regrow our company. And so there's a lot of power in this, you guys. This tool I'm giving you right now, this system, this whatever you want to call it, will serve you for the rest of your life if you just do it. And so at the event, a lot of people were asking me, when do I use this webinar? We talked about different things. We talked about an invisible funnel webinar. We talked about other ones. This webinar is a tool that you will use. It's a free webinar. People do not pay for this. You want this to be free because you want as many people as possible on it. And then you're going to give your presentation and then at the end you sell a product. So before we get into the actual script here, earlier one of our students was asking about what they sell. And so the biggest thing is when I'm selling on a webinar, I'm usually pricing the product I'm selling on a webinar. I usually am pricing somewhere between $500 up to about $2,500. That's kind of the range. So we talked about sales funnel. We talked about a lot of our front-end sales funnel, right? They're usually selling free plus shipping up to maybe $100 products. And then after I want to go higher price point, this is when I usually start shifting to webinars where I go again from $500 to $2,500. And then if I want to go above $2,500, that's when I start shifting to back-end phone sales and all the stuff we actually taught on high-ticket secrets. That's kind of the way that I structure this with what I'm doing. So if I'm selling anywhere from $500 to $2,500, boom, this script is the webinar I'm using. And so for a lot of you guys, if you don't have a product yet that kind of fits that, the first step is to figure out what's your offer going to be. What's the product you're going to sell people. The exercise I went through with one of our inner circle members yesterday, I was talking to her about her webinar. I said, look, what I would do, what we did is we always sit down in front of a whiteboard and we looked like if someone, you know, if I needed to make $1,000 today, I had to sell somebody on it, what would be the best possible thing I would sell that person? And we get out of whiteboard and we start mapping out all the ideas. I would do this and I would do this and I just, and we try to like turn off our filters. We put out everything like no matter how crazy it is, like I'm going to let them fly to my house and I'm going to feed them sushi. I'm going to let, you know, whatever, however you make this offer, this product, this thing they're going to buy, the most amazing thing in the world. You want to craft that out and put it out there, right? And you got to break it down. As you'll see when we come down here to like the stack, we'll break it down and there'll be pieces. But typically it's some kind of core training program, like six to eight modules. We're going to be teaching somebody a system, right? Your system, the thing that you are so good at doing. And module number one is going to be the first step, right? I always imagine like somebody just hired me and they're going to come to my office. I mean, what would I teach them over six days? Okay, module number one will be the first day I'm going to teach them. Module two will be the second day and then the third day. At the end of this week period of time, they're going to have my, they're going to know my system. They're going to understand what I do, right? And so that's why I look at what I'm creating. My initial thing is that is what's the core training system. And after that, then what are the bonuses I can get people that just make this a slam dunk where it makes it so irresistible. They have to have it. Okay. And we'll walk this little smiley face guys are here's is kind of that those, those elements, those bonuses that are so good. So you'll see as I go through, I take a secret. But hopefully it gives you some ideas and start thinking about it as you're creating an offer that's just irresistible. So people just have to say yes. They can't say no. Right. All right. Okay. So let me, let me go through this. So perfect webinar. So the webinar, there's basically, there's basically three sections of the perfect webinar. The first is your introduction. The second is the content section. And then the third is your clothes. Okay. And this is kind of how I, how I structure these. All right. Okay. So I'll begin with the, the introduction. Now the introduction is, is probably the easiest part, but the biggest key to the introduction, you guys, is you can't be boring. Okay. And people typically can't see you. If you're doing, if you're speaking from stage, then people will see you as you, as you use this script. Okay. And this is the same script I use when I'm speaking from stage to sell as they do on a webinar. But on a webinar, people can't see you. Okay. So the biggest thing is like your tonality, your voice is what keeps people around. If you talk like this and you're, you're kind of quiet, nobody will buy your product. Okay. If you look at me right now, I'm literally sitting on the edge of my seat. I have my hands in front of me and I'm moving them around and I, my voice goes up really, really high and it goes down. And I have to have tonality. I have to be exciting. I have to get people engaged. Okay. If you watch my webinars, you notice that what's interesting is, um, I learned this first time when I was, uh, I, we did an infomercial a little while ago. And, uh, for the infomercial, I remember, um, the guy, the infomercial producer told me, said, look, Russell, if you talk like you normally do on TV, you're going to sound like this. He said, if you talk super excited and kind of hype it over the top, you're going to sound normal. And it's the same on a webinar. You have to come with more energy than you do in your real world. Otherwise people are going to zone out and they're going to fall asleep. Okay. So I'm like at a level 10 right now, just talking to you guys. I'm not even selling you something right now, but if I was, it's even more important. Okay. So starting with a lot of energy and being exciting. Usually about a minute or two before the webinar starts, I like getting on and just saying, Hey, everyone, this is the webinar. We're going to be talking about high ticket secrets. You're just going to be awesome. Uh, we're about two minutes away from getting started. You're going to love it. Hey, everyone, if you're just, if you're just logging in right now, my name is Russell Brunson. We're here on the high ticket secrets webinar where we can start in about a minute 30 and you're going to love it. It's going to be awesome. So excited to have you guys here. Hey, everyone, this is Russell. We'll be, you know, and I keep looping that thing because people are always calling in. And so you can't just like, Hey guys, this is Russell. We'll get started in a minute. Hey guys, this is Russell. We'll get started in 30 seconds. I get to have constant energy. So I'm always, I just keep repeating myself like, like a, like a broken loop over and over again. Cause it keeps the energy up. It keeps people excited. And whenever they're dialing in and answering, they're hearing me talk and hearing that excitement and they feel like they're at something cool. Okay. Get some excited. And usually I'll even run that over maybe three or four minutes past when the webinar is supposed to start. I'll be like, Hey guys, um, webinars, you know, the webinar officially started about a minute ago, but I'm not going to start because people are still logging in. I'm going to give them one or two more minutes, but I want you to let you guys know you're in the right spot. I'm about to show you exactly how you can instantly increase your high ticket sales, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, right. And I keep recapping that to about two or three minutes in and now I'm going to start. Okay. That's key. So it's kind of my intro. Now I lead initially with a bold promise. Okay. This bold promise is going to tie back here to what I call the one thing, but the bold promise is kind of like, um, uh, I call it a ruler. Okay. It's, it's like someone's, everyone's measuring you all the time. Right. And if I come on a webinar, I start teaching, they're going to measure me versus every other webinar they've ever been on in the past. And they've been on people that are awesome on webinars. That's what they're going to judge me against. That's what the ruler is that they're going to say, was that a good webinar or a bad? It's all going to be based on the ruler they're using. Okay. And so that, I don't want to have them judge me based on just my presentation skills alone because again, I'm going to talk too fast. I'm going to slur things. Um, I want to judge, I want them to judge me based on what I'm promising. So I'm saying, this is my big promise. So for me, here's my big promise. I said, Hey guys, I'm going to show you how to instantly add high ticket sales to your sales funnel without you ever personally talking on the phone to anyone ever. Okay. At the end of this webinar, I'm going to, you're going to learn exactly how to do that. And it's sold. And this webinar will be a benefit success. Okay. Boom. Big, huge promise. And that's not what they're judging it on. They're judging the entire webinar based on if this, this promise, I just made them, if it's true. Okay. Then if they're like, yeah, Russell taught me, he taught me exactly how to build a sales funnel without personally talking on the phone to anyone ever. Boom. Okay. That's the ruler they're judging me against is perfect. Okay. Next, I'm going to hook them to the end. So again, my goal is if you stay to the end of this webinar, I'm going to do something really, really cool. Okay. So what I did for, for, for this audience, I said, you know, we're talking about call center. I said, like, if you guys stay till the end, I'm actually going to, I'm going to open up my laptop, put on my webcam. I'm walking and you can actually see my call center. You guys want to see that? People are going crazy. They're so excited. Okay. You only get that if you stay till the end. Now we've hooked them. Okay. Because during the first four or five minutes of webinars, when like 90% of your people leave, those that are going to leave, you're going to pass that first little hump. They're going to stay in the entire 90 minutes. Okay. So you got to hook them fast. You got to hook them with a bonus, with the prize, with free something with, you know, I've seen people give away iPads and give away phones and just some kind of hook you have to have. Okay. So it could be anything, just something to hook them to the end. Okay. And then I start commanding attention. Usually I say something like, Hey guys, if you got your cell phone out, if you got Skype, Facebook, you got to close it down, we need you. I need your attention. Okay. If you're not paying attention, none of this will help you at all. So you need to make sure you're paying attention. I'm trying to command attention. Then I qualify myself. So look, this is why I actually know what I'm talking about. Okay. And I share for this one, I showed a picture of me sleeping just to be funny, kind of a pattern interrupt. And then I came back and said, look, I'm, I don't talk about us a lot, but I'm the king of high ticket sales. I did back in sales for Dan Kennedy, for Bill Glazer, for Mike Filz, for Ray and Kern, plus a ton of other marketers. We've done over eight figures of high ticket sales. There's nobody in the marketplace better than me at high ticket sales. And I'm going to show you exactly how we do it. Boom, I just qualified myself. Okay. You guys got to qualify yourself. Why is what you do? Why are you qualified to do this? Okay. Why are you different than everybody else who's kind of teaching something like that? You got to qualify yourself and stick it out there so that people, they kind of get that. And then from there, I liked the future pace. And then I said, well, here's, some of these slides are still me qualifying. So here's my qualification. I did a lot of things right and a bunch of things wrong. I've been doing this for 10 years. This is kind of what I figured out. And this is kind of leading back to my content. I just got out of the hard way, but you're going to have a chance to cheat. Again, here's the hard way I did it. It was really hard and frustrating. Boom. And then I, this is where I switched to the future pacing. Okay. I say, look, so future pacing guys, just, you know, future pacing is I try to put them in a state where they can visual, they can visualize what will happen when they've accomplished this thing. Okay. So if you ever heard me, if you ever heard me say on a webinar or anything, I'm like, can you guys imagine what it's going to be like someday when your business is up and running and you're making your first thousand dollars like, wouldn't that be awesome? So I'm doing, I'm doing future pacing. Okay. So it's a, it's a concept from NLP where I'm trying to take you to a place in the future where you've already achieved your goals. Okay. So if I can do that, I get it's part of the intro. So right here I said, look, do you guys want an easy way that's going to require you to never pick up a phone? Can you imagine that? Like making high ticket sales out every afternoon, pick up the phone ever. Like what would you do with your life? What would you do different? Okay. What would in a month from now, when you're doing that, like what, what's it going to be like? What's your, what's your spouse going to say? What are your kids going to think when all of a sudden their dad's at home and they're making twice as much money? It's pretty exciting. You know what you think? Okay. Boom. I'm future pacing him. All right. Pushing him out into the future. All right. Then I kind of go back. This is just more, again, the script is, the script is, is a framework and I add things in between the, just I want to, right? So sometimes this one, I just kind of bragged a little bit. Last 14 days, there were over six figures in high ticket products. I didn't pick up the phone. And then I like throwing in some kind of like FTC disclaimer, like results are not typical. This is not going to work, blah, blah, blah, just to keep yourself kind of safe. Now I shift over here to, to the content part of the webinar. Okay. Now I want to step back. One of the biggest problems that people make when they're creating a webinar or any sales piece for that matter, it can be sales videos, sales letters, webinars, everything is because they're so in love with their product or their service and there's so many facets of it. They try to, they try to like sell something at every single thing that their thing can do, right? All the different, all the big, all the big, the big benefits of it. In fact, one of our inner circle members, I was just reviewing his sales video and he's crushing it. I think it did a million bucks last year with their sales video, but I said the biggest problem with your sales video right now, the reason why you're converting it one or two percent versus five or six percent is because you're selling four or five different things. People have to believe five different things you want to buy your product. Okay. I learned this originally from Perry Belcher. He talked about, he said that when he had sales letters that, that tried to get people to believe in two or more things, they always flopped. He said, he said, if I had sales letters that just try to get somebody to believe one thing, one core thing, if I did that, then they were successful. Okay. And I saw the exact same thing happen with me, you guys. I cannot tell you how many times all the webinars I've done where I try to like, okay, to get somebody to buy this, they need to believe in this, this, and this. So I structure where I'm trying to teach them all these things and you can't do it that way. That'll destroy your sales. Okay. It's the way, the reason why when I first did this, it didn't work. I get on stage, I'm going to show you my seven best things at how I make money. I show up seven things and they're like, wow, I got a lot of value out of that, but I got seven things I got to go do now and they would never buy, right? So you have to figure out from your entire presentation, from everything you do, what's the one thing? Okay. What is that one thing that is, is going to change and just make the, like the, the one thing they got to have. They have that one thing. It'll all make sense. Okay. If you look back here to my title, this was my one thing. Okay. One thing I learned from Dagen Smith, who was powerful, he said that if you're one thing, you're one thing should always be something like this. So it should be how to blank without blank. Okay. So you do that too and then insert like what they want. Okay. So for the audience I was talking to, they all want high ticket sales. Everyone online right now is talking about high ticket sales. So how do you, how to instantly add high ticket sales to your sales funnel? Okay. That's what they want. But what, what do they not want? Okay. For my audience, they don't want to pick up the phone. Okay. So you do that without personally talking on the phone to anyone ever. Can you guys see that? So for your market, what is it? How to lose a ton of weight overnight without giving up carbs. Okay. Do you see how that works? So that's usually what my one thing is tied to that. How to without, if you can, if you can figure out how to fit it into that little template, a little formula, it's magic. Okay. So that's my one thing. So what's key about this is that this entire webinar has to be the entire webinar is focused on that one key point. Okay. And everything, every single thing, every single thing else I talk about, the only goal of those things is to reemphasize over and over and over again, this one thing. Okay. Does that make sense? You guys. Okay. So I'm not going to be like, I'm going to show you this and this and this and this. I'm showing you this and then all these secrets, they tie back and they strengthen and they solidify that one thing. Okay. So again, here's my one thing. That was my one thing for this presentation. I come down here and I'm going to reveal the three secrets. These are my three coaching points on this. Okay. So secret number one is I can make more money in one day selling a high ticket products than I can in an entire month selling normal products. Okay. So that's my big secret. That's my first. I'm going to show you my big, my big thing I want to reveal to them. Okay. But what is it? Is that a new thing? No. All it's doing is it strengthening the one thing. Okay. It's showing them how they can make high ticket sales without picking up a phone. So secret number one is that you can make more in one day selling a high ticket sales and entire month selling normal products. Secret number two for me now was personally, I don't have to sell any or I don't, I personally have to sell anything because, which is a big plus because I hate the phones. Okay. So the second secret I'm going to show them is how they can do this without them ever picking up the phone. And then my third big secret is that you only need a little bit of traffic to make that work. Okay. Most of everyone is freaking out because it's going to take a ton of traffic and all this stuff to make this work. You only need a little bit of traffic to make this whole thing, this whole thing sing. Okay. So those are my three secrets. So I showed them up front like, hey guys, do this webinar. Okay. I told you that we're going to talk about how to, how to sell high ticket things without picking up the phone. Now to do that, I'm going to show you three, my big three secrets. Okay. Secret number one is this, secret number two is this, secret number three is this. So I kind of tell them what it is and I'm going to break into the content. Okay. Now I'm going to show you secret number one. So I come in and I say, this is what secret number one is. I can make more money in one day selling high ticket products than I can in an entire month selling normal products. Okay. Now notice that my secrets are kind of, they're typically backwards from what, from what most people are thinking or what most people believe. Okay. I'm trying to, I'm trying to break a belief pattern. That's why down here you see break and rebuild their belief patterns. Okay. So most of the people I'm talking to, they're selling ebooks, they're selling network marketing opportunities or selling low ticket stuff. And so I got to break that first paradigm, that first belief pattern. Okay. So what I do is I got to break it. So the first thing is I break, I break this pattern. Okay. I come and say, look, high ticket secrets, you know, excuse me, I say, look for typical price, you're selling $37 product and you want to make $5,000 a month, you got to sell 135 products. Okay. 3% conversion rate, that's 4,500 clicks. Okay. That's really, really hard. All right. And then I talk about to make $5,000 in selling a high ticket thing, I just got to sell one. Or if I'm selling $25,000 thing, I just got to sell one fifth. Every five months I got to sell one to make the exact same amount of money as it took you to make $37 product. Okay. So my goal is to break their belief pattern. Okay. I got to break this thing. You think that you have to sell $37 products. I got to break that thing and then I got to rebuild it with a pattern I want you to believe. Okay. And the pattern that I want them to believe for this webinar, what's my pattern I want them to believe? I want them to believe what's my one thing. They can do high ticket sales. That's my one thing. High ticket sales, high ticket sales. That's my one thing. Right. Boom, boom, boom. Okay. Now I switch over to secret number two. Secret number two is I don't personally have to sell anything because I hate the phones. Okay. Most of these guys who see people do high ticket stuff, they're like, high ticket stuff seems awesome, but I got to pick up the phone and I don't want to pick up the phone. I'm scared to talk on the phone to people. So that's their belief pattern. So what I got to do. I got to break that belief pattern and I got to rebuild it. So for me, I try to break it this way. So here's three reasons like people like me should never, ever pick up, pick up the phone. Okay. First of all, I'm way too busy. I should, I don't even want to answer my own support. I'm scared to death to be on the phone and it's just bad positioning. Okay. Most of these guys are like, yeah, I'm too busy too. Yeah. I'm scared. Oh, bad positioning. Sweet. Yeah. I don't want to talk to them anyway. Okay. I'm, I'm justifying their fears. Right. So I'm breaking that belief pattern because they've always believed that they have to be on the phone. And then I start, I start rebuilding it. So let me rebuild it. This is all you really need is two commission based salespeople. You guys know what commission based means? It means you only pay them when they make you money. Notice I said just two people, not 60. Okay. In the past, I had 60 people and it was a nightmare, but I figured out a new way to do it. And this is how it worked. And I talked about this experiment and then this is the old model, how we did a new model. So again, I'm breaking this pattern and I'm rebuilding it. Okay. This, and I'm taking them on the journey that I went through to rebuild. Cause I had the same pattern in my head. That's why I had 60 salespeople working for me. Okay. And then I went on the journey I went through to break that. I had to go through to break my own pattern and how I rebuilt it and I'm taking them on that journey so that I can help break it with them and rebuild it with them. Boom. So I experiment. Here's how it worked and what happened and how we made 36,000 bucks in a day. And I would grew from there and we got two salespeople and boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, boom. And then I say, Hey, you want to meet Robbie? And this is now where I do, where I went back to my initial hook. Okay. Where I did a hook to the end. I was like, you guys want to see, you want to see the mini call center and I put on my laptop webcam mic and I walked him into the other room. Now typically I would do this at the end of the webinar, but for this one it just tied into secret number two. So I needed to do it then. Okay. All right. So there's secret number two. And notice like I'm teaching them cool stuff. I'm not really teaching them anything. That makes sense. Okay. But you're learning cool things because you're, cause what's happening is I'm breaking these belief patterns and I'm rebuilding them. And so while I'm not giving you any like here's step one, two, three on how to do anything, you're learning your, your, your, your, your paradigm has been shifted, which people they, they resonate with that. That feels like learning to them. Right. And so, so don't feel guilty. Don't feel like you're not going to be teaching them stuff because you are teaching them, but you're doing it in a different way that I think is a better way to shift somebody's paradigm and shift the way they believe is way more powerful than here's step one, step two, step three. Okay. So you're teaching in a different way. It's a way that sets up the sale. Okay. All right. Now for me, I moved to secret number three, which is I only need a little bit of traffic to make this work about a hundred clicks. Because what, what do most people think? I would love to do high ticket things, but I can't drive traffic. I can't send a thousand clicks a day. You know that in their mind, it's got to be this huge thing. I take him to a case study page, then a homework page is how it works. Now if I sell high ticket products, this is how I'd be, you know, here's all I said 4500 clicks to make my four, my $5,000 a month. Plus I got to spend money on those clicks. I didn't make it almost nothing. If you're selling high ticket things, check this out. If I sell a $5,000 product, one out of ten to apply, or get a sign up for a $5,000 product, one out of ten to visit the application page you're going to apply, so 100 clicks equals 100 applications equals 100 buyers. That means all I'm going to do is send 100 clicks a day and I can make five grand a day. Is that awesome? And I start future pacing. Imagine if you're selling $10,000 or $25,000 or $100,000. Think how much money that is. And imagine if you, instead of sending 100 clicks a day, what if you send 200 clicks a day? What if you send 300 clicks a day? Boom, and I teach them, right? All right. So there's the content. So you guys see how I structure that? It's all about, you know, it's all like script one, two, and three, all they're doing is supporting the one thing, the one core focus of this entire thing. It's not new content. It's not the next thing. It's not anything else. Because I promise you, if ten of you guys make a webinar and you come back to me, 90 you guys are going to do this wrong. You're going to have the one thing, which is your, the first thing you're excited to teach, and it's going to be the next thing, and the next thing, you'll be awesome teachers, and people are going to love what you're going to share, but it's not going to, it's not going to result in sales at the end. This is how you have to structure your content to result in sales at the end. Okay. All right. Now, from here, this is where we transition to, to the sale. Okay. And this is where most people start getting really, really, really nervous. The other day I was at this, someone that goes to church to me, they said, hey, we're doing this little party at our house. You want to come on over? My wife got all excited. So it's this party I show up and it was an MLM party. Now for me, I'm super fired up and excited. I love seeing people sell stuff. I love MLMs. And so they're pitching this thing, right? So they get up in this little room with like 10 people and I got a big old grin on my face and everyone else is like all nervous because they think they're getting pitched. And I'm so excited. And the person's talking and they're showing the, it was essential oils and showing the oils how they work. And the content was amazing. Like you could tell they were so fired up and passionate. And all of a sudden about an hour into it, the lady was speaking. I see her start talking faster and she started talking faster and she starts getting nervous and her face turns red and she starts sweating and she starts mumbling and all these things start happening. And I just start laughing because I know she's getting so close to wanting to sell me something but she's so scared. And all of a sudden she went, oh, and by the way, you can make money a little, a little, a little, a little, a little, a lot. And then she, she ended. And if you want any more questions, then my husband's over there and ended. And it was the most awkward thing in the world. And, uh, and that's how most people do when they, they, they love the teaching part and you transition to sell, they get scared, they get nervous, they get whatever that is, right? And that happens to all of us. Um, I've been on tons of webinars where people don't know how to transition from that to the next. And so in this webinar, I didn't use it, but, um, traditionally this is the, this is the, um, excuse me, this is the, uh, the way I transition. And for you guys, I, I would say this is probably the first webinar I haven't transitioned this way. And if I was to redo this webinar, this would be the one thing I would change about it is this transition. It's just, it works. So I finished teaching the cons, the content right. I teach this last thing about how much traffic and you know, I show them, imagine doing this, and they're kind of in this future state where they're excited about the future. And I stop and I say, let me ask you a question. This stuff's been pretty cool, right? You, you've had a lot, we've had a good time over the last hour. You've learned some good stuff, but, but an hour is not, not that long. Like how would you guys like to work with me directly and why I can actually help you implement what we just talked about? Boom. That's it. Let me ask you a question and kind of talk about everything that you just went over. How would you like help on this? Cause this gets all of them are thinking at this point. I just do this whole future pacing about them making 10 or 25 or 100 grand. They're so excited. But then they get that, that feeling where it's like, wow, I don't know how to do that. It's gonna be a lot of work. And that's when you come in and save the day and you say, look, let me ask you a question. All that stuff we just covered, it's exciting. All right. You just imagine, imagine a hundred K. Like, what, like, what would that do for you? What would they do for your life? Okay. It's pretty cool, right? Let me ask this question. Like how, how would it help you? Like as your teacher, as your coach, whatever, I want to serve you. Like, how would you like if I could actually help you implement this? And guess everyone comes back and says, they come back in the chat box and they think, yes, yes, please, I want help. I love help. Let me, how can you help me? That helped me. And then I like asking permission like, okay, well, a lot of you guys want information. I've got something I can do that can help you. Okay. Now I know it's always awkward. Like you're on this webinar and you know, I, I do have something to sell. And if you guys are okay with that, I'd love to share it with you. If not, I won't. But if you want, if you want me to share it with you, let me know. I'd be like, yeah, share me, share, share. And they, they start asking you to, to sell them. Okay. And it becomes really easy after, after you've transitioned past that, right? Now from there, I, I transition over to what I call the stack. Okay. Now you notice the stack, you won't see it for a little bit here because, because the stack now is the framework that the rest of the presentation takes. And then these closes are closes that you sprinkle in in, in between the stack. That makes sense. So let me tell you what the stack is. So typically when, and I learned this from Armin Moore, and I will give him a 100% credit, he's made me like literally the stack has made me over $10 million in direct sales. I can tie it back to this concept. I'm going to teach you right now called the stack. Uh, it was before I spoke my first time speaking Armin's event and it was two weeks before we were at a dad, Kenny event. Armin got up there and he spoke to an audience of 1000 people and he closed like, I don't know, it was insane, like 30 or 40 or 50% of the audience. They ran back and he, he did like, I don't know. He was just shy of a million dollars in sales by himself. And I remember watching it. I was just like, that was the coolest thing ever. And afterwards he came up to me with this huge stack of order forms in his hands. He's like, you want a bunch of order forms like this next week or two weeks when you speak in my event? Like, yeah, man, I totally do. Like what do I got to do? And he said, um, I got to teach us a little process called the stack. He's like, if you do it, you'll get a big stack of order forms. You don't do it. You're not going to make much money. So that's just how it works. I'm like, all right, well, how does it work? He said, this is the big secret. He said, most of the presenters, you watch the way they do their presentation. They'll say, Hey, the first thing you're going to get is this, and then you're going to get this, and then you're going to get this. He keeps walking through all the stuff they're going to get. And then the last slide, they're like, this is bonus number 12, the sweet you're going to get. And then they go over, they go over to the next slide. We're like, I'm going to give you all this stuff for, you know, $10,000. And then they start doing the price drop. Now what happens is the only thing that the human mind remembers is the last thing they were shown. So right before, he's like, right before you showed them this big price, the only thing he remembers, bonus number 10 or 12, whatever your last thing is you showed, and that bonus doesn't justify that price in their mind, then they're not going to buy it. He said, so I invented this thing called the stack. He said, this is how it works. You can kind of see here. He says, I start my presentation, I stack like the first component, and I talk about it. And then, actually, I'll show you guys how it works down here. So when I did the stack, first thing I did is I showed right here, I said, let me talk about what you're going to get. Okay, first thing you're going to get is this. So I did the first stack. Second thing you're going to do is you're going to get this. And third thing you're going to do is this. Okay, if I was clicking through these, you're animated. Fourth thing you're going to do is this. Boom. So this is the total value 8,000 bucks. So this is the core offer. Okay, so if any of you guys, if any of you guys are, like if you have your core thing, you're teaching somebody, some of you guys are going to be six modules or it's going to be, you know, four modules teaching. That's what this is. This is my core offer, right? I'm going to show you how to set up a two-set funnel, help me set up mini call center. I'm going to give you the actual script for use. I'm going to give you Robbie in the box. Boom, there's the core offer, right? Now I'm going to start adding value. That's these little smiley face guys are adding value, right? So I'm going to give you this bonus, which is unlimited traffic. Boom. And I'm going to give you this bonus, which is our ads or interviews. Okay. So what that means is now I stack this. That means you're going to get and recap the first thing I told them, the second thing, the third thing, the fourth thing. So I just, I just reset that. And I say, plus you're going to get bonus number one, which is unlimited traffic, which is the $1,909 value. And you're going to get bonus number two, which is hiring commissions and more for $997. So now the total value of this package is $11,998. You guys have probably seen me do this before, right? You've seen other presenters. Okay. It's kind of cheesy, right? But I go through and I start doing some of these other closes and other closes, other closes. And then I come back and I add another bonus. Okay. I'm going to give you this 30 minute consult with Robbie, which is the $2,000 value. And I come back, boom, I get the guarantee, guarantee, boom, and then I stack it again, which means you're going to get the two-step funnel. You're going to get this mini call center value of $2,000. And literally, you guys, by the time you've done the stack like five or six times, you're going to be so embarrassed. And I remember first time I did this at a seminar, people were laughing and it got kind of like ridiculous. But guess what? Everybody knew exactly what the offer was and they bought like crazy. So I don't not ever do this because it works so good. Okay. Component number three, I'm going to give you this. I'm going to give you this, which means you're also going to get this and you're going to get this. And like I just told you, I'm going to add in this bonus as well. It's total value now is this price. Isn't that awesome? And I dropped in some more closes, more closes, boom, recap again. So I'm going to stack it again and then I give them the price and then they can buy it. Okay. So that's kind of the, the form of how the stack works. Okay, guys. Those who were in Boise, Natasha, Natasha was there and I taught Natasha the stack probably probably two years ago. And she's one of my favorite people. She learned something. She applies it. She did the first webinar and she crushed it and she did it again. She crushed it. She's like, I feel so cheesy, but I just do it because it works every single time. She just killed it over and over and over again. And then she did her very first live event here in Boise and she had a room, literally, I think her room had maybe 30 or 40 people. It was a smaller event and she, at the end of the three day event or two day event, she did this, she did this presentation. I used to sell her high end coaching and she did the stack and she actually had huge, uh, huge boards up on the wall that had the stack. But everything was kind of covered up. And one time she pulled the thing off, pulled the second thing off and she did the whole thing and boom, she did her stack and she made fit. She sold, uh, 10 people at five grand, 50 or $50,000 in sales from one presentation to a group of like 30 people by using the stack. And she swears by it. She won't ever not do it. I'm the same way. Okay. After Armin showed to me, I added my presentation. I went and did his event and boom, I crushed. I was the number one sales person in the event. I walked away with like almost a hundred grand in sales and I was like, this thing is awesome. And I just have used it every single time, religiously, since then. Actually, that's not true. Once I thought I was too smart and I didn't use it and I bombed. I still remember the webinar. I was so embarrassed. Um, I was doing it for some guy and I thought I had a better idea than the stack and I did it and webinar bombed and I've never got, I've put it back in. I've never not done it since then. Okay. So that's the webinar worksheet. Here's component number one. Boom. Talk about component one. Here's component number two, which means you're going to get this and this. Talk about that. You know, add testimonials. These little spidey faces are, are bonuses, testimonials, inserting closes, just how much of stuff to strengthen fortified that next component. Then you restack it, share the next thing, restack it, share the next thing, restack it and then boom. After you restack it all, now it's when you come to the big price. Okay. That means that total price of all this stuff. What did I do that on? The total price is somewhere. Boom. Okay. The total price is $11,998. I show the big, huge price. Now, most people, they do that and they get scared. Like, Oh, but don't worry guys. I'm not going to charge you that. And what happens? You just lost your credibility. That's one of the biggest mistakes I see, I see a webinar people do. They show the big price and then they freak out. Don't worry guys. I'm not going to charge you that. And they move on. That's like the worst thing. And this is again, this is the tactic I learned from, from Dustin and Dave is as soon as I revealed a huge price, right? I show the $11,000 thing. Now I need to make that price. I need to justify that price in their head. If I don't justify that price in their head, then they're not, then, then when I actually sell the product, it's not going to work. I have to get them mentally to believe that it's actually worth that. And if I don't, then I lost the sale. Okay. So after I show this high ticket thing, this now is where, um, this is where I actually add my if all statements. Okay. I learned this from, from Dave van Hoos. So I, I, I set the high price and I say, look guys, I'm not, I'm not going to charge $11,998 today. I could, but I'm not going to, but I want to ask you something. And then this is where you add your if all. If all that this package did was blank, would it be worth it? If all this, if, if all this package did was finally make your business profitable, would it be worth $11,000? Yeah, I thought so. Now, if all this package did was finally give you the kick in the butt, you need to get moving, would it be worth it? I think so too. Now, if all this package did was give you two to three high ticket clients per month, that'd be worth it. Yeah. I don't know about you, but I, I twidled $12,000 for two or three, you know, five to $10,000 sales a month all day long. This is one time investment. You do it once, you never have to pay again. But that knowledge, the information, everything you got will pay you forever. Okay. So these if all statements, they solidify this price in their mind. That makes sense. You guys, if alls. And now I come back to say, but don't worry guys, I'm not going to charge you that. In fact, what I'm going to do is, and you can start dropping the price. Okay. I went a little bit further because I talked about, about two or three clients. What's that worth to him? And I said, and I want to ask you another question. What's one client going to be worth to you? If your package of five grand is worth 5,000, 10,000, 25,000 or more. So how much would you pay to get one high ticket client per month? How about one per month per week? How much per day? What would you pay for that? Okay. You probably would pay 12,000 bucks if I could show you that, right? It's a good deal. But don't worry. Oh, and then I also like, we had a couple people who pay us a million dollars to have something similar. So I, again, send the value like, we've had people pays over a million dollars for this literally, and you're getting it for a fraction. So don't worry. I'm not going to price it at five grand. Um, I'm not going to do it for 2,000. I'm going to do it for a thousand. So price dropped down. Now that I, sweet, here's a, here's a really good deal. Okay. So disposable income. This is where, um, you, you talk about the, the fact that like, like all of us have disposable income. Like most of you guys, you get your paycheck each month and a percentage that goes towards, towards movies and towards things like that. And, uh, and I'm not asking you guys to, to go and go into debt for this at all. What I'm trying to ask you guys to do is that some of that disposable income that you're going to spend on the movies and, and, and, and going out to dinner and all that kind of stuff, instead of blowing it on something that's just going to be there and then kind of fade away and you're going to lose it. You, I, I'm asking you to make an investment yourself because the money you put into this is going to turn into more money. Okay. A lot of times like talking about, I might talk about Robert Kiyosaki here. Like you guys have read the, read the book, rich dad, poor dad. Now in the book, rich dad, poor dad, he talked about difference between assets and liabilities. He said that the poor people, they buy liabilities. Okay. They buy cars. They buy, they buy boats. They buy these things. You put money in and the money disappears. Okay. Um, what the rich people do is they put their money into investments that actually turn into more money. So all I'm doing is to ask you to divert some of your disposable income into an, into an investment as opposed to something that's going to take money away from you. Okay. That's kind of how I use, how I do the disposable income close. Okay. Money replenish this close. This one is one of my, of my other favorite ones. And we got a 25% increase in sales from adding that little thing in there. From all those people who, who they're in, they've got one question. It's magic. Okay. So that's a really good one. And the last one, um, this one's kind of a bigger thing, uh, kind of a higher, an overall hierarchy principle. Um, I learned this initially from a guy named Ted Thomas. They call him the Pied Piper because when he speaks at like a Robert Kiyosaki event, I've seen pictures of him. Well, he'll literally be walking around the room and he'll have a line of like 500 people all holding their credit card, following him behind him, waving their credit cards, and they'll fall into the back of the room. And, uh, it's, uh, it's pretty awesome. Anyway, they called him the Pied Piper because of that. And I met him at an event. He saw me speak. And then afterwards he pulled me aside. He said, Russell, you did, you did great. You did sales. We're good. He said, do you know this one thing? He said, when, when you were, uh, when you were speaking, so I was sitting in the back of the audience and you did a great job. You know, people signed up. They were excited, but I noticed nobody's heads were moving. You said, when you come to my audience and you watch me speak, you said, and you're sitting in the back of the room. It looks like you're watching a wave. So everybody's heads are nodding up and down the entire 90 minutes. Okay. And he said, you said, you know how I do that? I said, no, how do you do it? He said, I do it by this little thing called trial closes. You know, all the trial closes is something you, I throw out there to get somebody to say yes. Does that make sense? Okay. I said, does that make sense? Cause that was a trial close. You guys know, it's probably notice I say that a lot. Okay. I say, isn't that awesome? You guys getting this? Is this exciting? Like when you see how this all works and everyone listening at home saying, yeah, that is exciting. That is cool. And start nodding their head. Yes. Okay. So the first time I learned about these trial closes, I went back to an automated webinar I had and I literally went through and I rewatched the entire like three hour long webinar and I listened to every single spot. I could insert a trial close and I wrote this thing out and I had my brother record me. I record myself saying each of these old trial closes and I had my brother who does my video stuff, insert those trial closes in my entire three hour webinar. And then we started running it and our, um, our profit per, per webinar registrant went from $9 and like 40 cents a person up to $16. It was like almost, we almost doubled our income just by adding in these trial closes. It's crazy. Okay. Now for me, when I first started doing this, what I would do is I would take all these trial closes and I'd write them down on note cards and I put them all over my desk. And I was giving webinars. I looked down, I would say, I would get to a spot. I'd be like, isn't that awesome? Are you guys getting this? Okay. Do you guys see how this would help you in your life? Can you imagine what it'd be like if you were doing this every single day? Okay. And I started saying that all over the, all over the place. And now it's a point where if you guys just watch this webinar, I'm not even trying to close you, but I guarantee your head's been nodding. I've been dropping trial closes every like 10 seconds on you guys. It's just how I speak now. All right. And it's something you guys will learn as well, but trial closes are powerful and best place to add trial closes. Oh, besides the entire presentation, but anytime you share a success story or you share one of your big secrets, most people will share testimonial and then they move on. Can we share testimonial? You should drop five or six trial closes right afterwards. So I just showed how Susie did this thing as she made, she lost 30 pounds and then I'm going to stop and be like, isn't that awesome? What happened? Can you imagine if you lost 30 pounds right now? Would that be awesome? What would, what would, what would that mean for you in your life? If you lost 30 pounds, would your spouse look at you a little differently? Okay. Would your kids think about you differently? That'd be pretty great. Don't you think? Well, my shot five trial closes and then move on. Okay. Like, I don't know about you, but I'm scared about, are you guys scared about picking up the phones? Yeah, me too. Like, would that be great if you could do that? I just imagined how it would change your life. Okay. Some dropping these trial closes all over the place. Okay. So next time you guys watch one of my webinars, look for the trial closes.
